content.full_text:
MAR 18 '91 05:33PM SCAQMD                                                P.2


                    South Coast
              AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT
         9150 FLAIR DRIVE, EL MONTE, CA 91731    (818) 572-6200

                              March 18, 1991


VIA TELECOPIER

Mr. Gregory Baugher
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
Suite 800
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95804

                  Re: Request For Opinion On South Coast Air Quality Management
                      District's Designated Employees' Receipt Of An Honorarium

Dear Mr. Baugher:

       This letter is being sent to request an opinion from the Fair Political
Practices Commission ("FPPC") regarding receipt of an honorarium1/ by South
Coast Air Quality Management District ("District") employees designated under the
District's conflict of interest code. We have conferred with Ms. Blanca Breeze in
the FPPC's General Counsel office regarding the issues set forth below and, based
on our discussion, it appears that these issues are best addressed in a written
opinion. In addition, because the deadline for employees to file their Statement of
Economic Interests is April 1, and these issues are recurring regularly, we would
greatly appreciate it if the FPPC could expedite its preparation of this opinion.

       Specifically, we would like to receive the FPPC's opinion on what actions
District employees must take, if any, to comply with the Political Reform Act (Gov.
Code Sec. 81000 et seq.) under the following circumstances:

       1. Are designated employees subject to the requirements
       of receipt of an honorarium as set forth in Cal. Code Regs., tit.
       2, Sec. 18728(a)? This question is based upon the fact that the
       statutory reference for this section is Gov. Code Sec. 87207 and
       this statute section is inapplicable to these employees under
       Gov. Code Sec. 87200.


_____________________

       1/  For purposes of this letter, the term "honorarium" means a payment for
speaking at any event, or participating in a panel or seminar, or engaging in any
similar activity. In addition, the term should be construed to include free admission,
meals, travel (both intra- and interstate travel) and accommodations provided in
connection with the event, and other nominal benefits.


MAR 18 '91 15:35                                                 2894807   PAGE.002

MAR 18 '91 05:40PM SCAGMD P.3

Mr. Gregory Baugher
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 18, 1991
Page 2

If designated employees are subject to this regulation, then
is payment for speaking at an event or participating in a panel,
or payment for interstate travel and accommodations, or both,
considered to be either income, as that term is defined in Gov.
Code Sec. 82030, or a gift, as that term is defined under Gov.
Code Sec. 82028(a)? If it falls under either one or both of
these categories, what are the applicable disclosure
requirements for the recipient employee? Further, is
disqualification required if the appropriate financial thresholds
for direct and indirect involvement by a source of income or
gift are met?

2. If this regulation is inapplicable, then is receipt of an
honorarium considered to be income or a gift or both under the
above-referenced statutory definitions? If so, what are the
applicable disclosure and disqualification requirements for the
recipient employee?

In addition, are designated employees subject to Gov.
Code Sec. 89501 which limits the amount of honoraria that a
local elected official may accept from a single source? If so, on
what legal authority does the FPPC rely on for its conclusion?

3. Regardless whether Cal. Code Regs. tit. 2, Sec. 18728 is
applicable, if the FPPC considers an honorarium to be a gift,
does this require compliance with the disclosure requirements
set forth in Gov. Code Sec. 87313?

4. If the FPPC considers an honorarium to be income but
not because it is a gift, does receipt of such payments have to be
disclosed if, for example, under Gov. Code Sec. 82030(a) the
source of income is a trade association located outside the
District's boundaries and the association does not do any
business with the District? Does this conclusion change if the
association has members either located within the District or
subject to its regulations? Alternatively, does a trade
association qualify as a bona fide educational or academic
organization under Gov. Code Sec. 82030(b) and, if so, under
what circumstances? Further, if an association does qualify as
such an organization, does reimbursement for travel expenses,
regardless whether for intra- or interstate travel, and per diem
have to be disclosed by the recipient employee?

5. If a designated employee receives an honorarium from
a foreign government, including payment of travel expenses and
meals, does this have to be disclosed by the recipient employee
as income or a gift? Does the FPPC's conclusion change if the

MAR 18 '91 15:35 2894807 PAGE.003

MAR 18 '91 05:41PM SCAQMD                                                P.4


Mr. Gregory Baugher
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 18, 1991
Page 3


        source of income is a foreign organization such as a public
interest group representing environmentalists?

        If you believe it would be appropriate, we would be pleased to meet with you
or your counsel to discuss these issues further before an informal written opinion is
prepared.   Also, with regard to this written opinion, please provide specific
references to applicable statutes, regulations, case law, and FPPC opinions which
support your analysis. Please also provide copies of advice letters which are not
available in the FPPC opinions contained in the California Continuing Education of
the Bar notebooks.

        Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

                                          Sincerely,


                                          [signature]

                                          Ted M. Handel
                                          Senior Deputy District Counsel


TMH:pc
cc:    Ms. Bianca Breeze
       Fair Political Practices Commission




MAR 19 '91 18:36                                                2284307   PAGE 004
